question: What renders a Loom report by default — do I need Power BI?
expectedAnswer: The default is the Loom-native renderer: it queries the bound Azure Analysis Services tabular model with DAX and renders the rows — no Power BI or Fabric workspace, and api.powerbi.com is never reached on the default path. The live Power BI embed is opt-in via LOOM_BI_BACKEND=powerbi.

question: How do I opt into the Power BI embed report backend?
expectedAnswer: Set LOOM_BI_BACKEND=powerbi (client NEXT_PUBLIC_LOOM_BI_BACKEND); the editor then uses the live Power BI embed, which requires the Console UAMI to be registered in a Power BI workspace.

question: How do visuals render on the Loom-native report path?
expectedAnswer: A card visual runs a single DAX ROW query into a big-number tile; table/bar/line/pie run DAX TOPN(100) into a Fluent Table — chart types currently render as a table with an honest note that a charting lib is a follow-up. Refresh re-runs every visual's DAX query.

question: What happens when a report has no AAS binding?
expectedAnswer: The query route returns a 412 unbound code and the editor shows a warning MessageBar naming the binding (state.aasServer/state.aasDatabase or LOOM_AAS_SERVER/LOOM_AAS_DATABASE) while the full page and visual surface still render as a config-only preview.

question: How does the Loom-native report path authenticate to AAS?
expectedAnswer: As the Console UAMI with the AAD scope for asazure.windows.net (Gov: asazure.usgovcloudapi.net); the UAMI must be an AAS server admin. Visual rows come from the AAS data-plane models/{db}/query endpoint with DAX EVALUATE.

question: How does export to PDF/PPTX/PNG work on the Power BI path?
expectedAnswer: Via the real Power BI REST ExportTo flow: POST ExportTo, poll the export id, then download the file — wired to the Export menu on the opt-in embed path.

question: Are bookmarks supported in the embedded report?
expectedAnswer: Yes — the Bookmarks panel uses the embed JS API bookmarksManager (getBookmarks/apply/capture) and a Play/Stop slideshow via bookmarksManager.play, all against the live embed session.

question: What is the default semantic-model backend in Loom?
expectedAnswer: Azure Analysis Services, per the no-fabric-dependency rule — Power BI is opt-in. When the AAS bicep module is deployed, bicep sets the backend selector to aas and the editor renders the AAS panel; refresh routes dispatch to AAS by default.

question: How does Refresh now work for an AAS-backed semantic model?
expectedAnswer: It POSTs to the AAS async-refresh REST (models/{db}/refreshes) and reads the real refresh id from the Location header; refresh history lists the last 30 days from the same API.

question: Where is the scheduled refresh for an AAS semantic model stored?
expectedAnswer: As the loom-refresh-schedule ARM tag on the AAS server (PATCH/GET on the server resource) — enable, days, times, timezone, and notify settings persist there.

question: Can I send TMSL commands to the model?
expectedAnswer: Yes — createOrReplace/alter/refresh TMSL goes through the XMLA endpoint (SOAP Execute POST to servers/{name}/xmla) via the AAS client's command helper.

question: What gates the AAS semantic-model surface when env vars are missing?
expectedAnswer: When LOOM_AAS_SERVER_NAME / LOOM_AAS_REGION are unset the aas-databases route 503s with a gate and the editor renders a warning MessageBar naming the exact env vars plus the aas.bicep module. No Fabric or Power BI workspace is ever required.

question: What does the aas.bicep module provision?
expectedAnswer: An AAS Standard (S1) server with the Console UAMI added as server administrator (app:{clientId}@{tenantId}) for the refresh REST, a Reader grant for ARM reads, and diagnostics to the Log Analytics workspace; main.bicep wires the AAS env vars and sets the BI backend to aas.

question: What are the AAS endpoints per cloud?
expectedAnswer: Commercial/GCC use the asazure.windows.net data-plane suffix, GCC-High/IL5 use asazure.usgovcloudapi.net, and DoD derives from an override suffix; the auth audience and ARM base follow the same per-cloud split via cloud-endpoints.

question: How do report pages navigate on the Loom-native path?
expectedAnswer: The Pages panel (left tree) and the ribbon page picker call setActivePage; the definition (report, AAS binding, pages) loads from Cosmos state via GET /api/items/report/[id] with no workspaceId needed.
